summary: In patients with unexplained infertility despite having a healthy uterus and ovaries, endometriosis remains a leading differential because it impairs fertility through inflammatory mechanisms affecting follicle maturation and embryo implantation rather than relying solely on anatomical distortions. This makes it particularly plausible given the absence of other identifiable reproductive system pathologies.
